name: Blackened Chicken Strips on Pasta W/Cajun Sauce
recipeIngredient: 4 chicken breast halves without skin, boneless
1 tablespoon fresh herbs, chopped (like: oregano, basil, tarragon, marjoram)
recipeInstructions:
1. To make Cajun spice, mix paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, pepper, and fines herbes. Set aside.
2. Pound chicken breasts between 2 pieces of wax paper until flattened.
3. Moisten the flattened chicken breasts with water and rub them with Cajun spice (reserve 1/4 teaspoon for the sauce).
4. Spray a very hot cast-iron pan with vegetable spray and quickly fry the chicken breasts for 4 to 6 minutes, or until cooked through. Remove the breasts and slice into thin strips. Set aside.
5. In a small pan sprayed with vegetable spray, saute garlic and onion until soft. Add broth, tomato, wine, fresh herbs, and 1/4 teaspoon of Cajun spice. Simmer for 8 minutes. Season with salt to taste.
6. Meanwhile, cook pasta, drain, and place on a platter. Lay the chicken strips on the pasta and drizzle with the sauce.
